summary.summary: Adds scroll-to and highlight support for settings search results and fixes search-index label mappings. A new SettingsSearchHighlight observable, SearchResultTarget wrapper, and two view modifiers (.settingsSearchTarget(label:) and .settingsHighlightScroll()) were introduced in Trio/Sources/Views/SettingsSearchHighlight.swift to drive scroll-to and highlight behavior (file:a5bbf1102c8d, commit:aaccd911b543c016847535515cfc3481e3729fcc). Several settings sections that previously used .listRowBackground(Color.chart) were changed to call .settingsSearchTarget(label:) so they become scroll anchors (examples: DynamicSettingsRootView and UserInterfaceSettingsRootView) (file:957b2629117c, file:6e1eb5490a55, commit:6aab0ae5b05998c925bf89d88269b7fcaa728229). A .settingsHighlightScroll() modifier was added and applied to multiple settings destination views so a settings screen will scroll-to/highlight any incoming search target (commit:5cb78b6af0bae7751167527160d7f9232a9559b2; files changed include: file:4f729c9baaa8, file:dc7a7ed862df, file:dfc131bba77c, file:508a1c305d61, file:abcaffc13fe9, file:6d2ef85679e4, file:ae8fed6e342c, file:d222b9a826b9, file:3db70b1792a6, file:582b8d822258, file:d853c20fb4f8, file:5e336c89bcc4, file:e1019083befb, file:f2ae5b8b8307, file:a6bd926b0f4b, file:c69e71799ce6, file:94a2af006c29, file:feaf1d3e3add). The settings search index / SettingItem model was extended with an optional scrollTargetLabels mapping to map search terms to the exact UI label used as the scroll anchor; SettingItems and many mappings were added/adjusted to restore older search terms and point them at the correct anchors (file:4652ce349121, commit:6aab0ae5b05998c925bf89d88269b7fcaa728229, commit:d6920ef137efc77f4968b695a6229cb143417fec, commit:350680c3cfdcbbd4e423b3ab87c8edbb8b08981b). Settings.RootView was updated to navigate to a SearchResultTarget and set the highlight state on navigation so the destination view scrolls and highlights the matched section (file:f2ae5b8b8307, commit:6aab0ae5b05998c925bf89d88269b7fcaa728229). Unit tests validating search navigation, scrollTargetLabels, and the highlight state were added as TrioTests/SettingsSearchTests.swift and incorporated into the Xcode project (file:2ea6d8a8098e, file:6f9a35467e7c, commit:20b5bf4429649f5e54c34b393b45b3d83b82ba62). Several commits apply iterative fixes and code-review suggestions to timing/animation and mappings (commit:d1bd5368e0a8e8c8501202f6b3d9f5d049faa3f5, commit:350680c3cfdcbbd4e423b3ab87c8edbb8b08981b, commit:e23ff28a3ed5a575da3dcbce3c7c6170af928ed7, commit:61ed450625a750800caf9780f823d8f7653b1fb6, commit:b6bc8c067feaa581c8d46919a4e1ce9aa5a3ebc9).
summary.userFacingImpact: Settings search results can now navigate directly to the matching settings section and briefly highlight it; several search terms were remapped so older/abbreviated names scroll to the correct UI label (file:f2ae5b8b8307, file:4652ce349121, file:6e1eb5490a55). Tests were added to validate search-to-scroll behavior (file:2ea6d8a8098e). No changes outside settings search/navigation and UI highlighting are indicated by the supplied changes (commit:aaccd911b543c016847535515cfc3481e3729fcc, commit:6aab0ae5b05998c925bf89d88269b7fcaa728229).
